title: Grief Self-Care Checklist
description: A gentle daily or weekly check-in to help you notice whether your basic needs are being met during a very difficult time. This is not a test or a set of requirements. Some days, just getting through is enough.
instructions: If it feels possible, review these items and check off what you've managed. Even one or two checked items is meaningful. Be honest with yourself, and be kind to yourself about what's unchecked.

items.label: I had at least one nourishing food or snack today (or recently)
I drank water or other hydrating fluids several times
I took prescribed medications or attended medical appointments I need
I tried to rest or sleep, even if sleep was disrupted
I moved my body in a gentle way (short walk, stretching, standing outside)
I spent a few minutes in fresh air or natural light
I noticed a physical sensation (tension, heaviness, restlessness) and acknowledged it
I had at least one supportive contact (message, call, presence), even if brief
I accepted help from someone or asked for something specific
I was in the company of another person at some point (even briefly, even in silence)
I allowed myself to feel whatever came up without judging it as wrong
I allowed myself a moment of rest without judging myself for it
I spoke to myself with kindness (as I would to a dear friend)
I named what I was feeling, even just to myself (sad, angry, numb, grateful, scared)
I did not use alcohol or substances to numb my pain today
I paced myself — did a little, then rested, instead of pushing through

disclaimer: This checklist is for gentle self-reflection, not a standard to meet. Grief is exhausting, and some days basic survival is the goal. If you're struggling to care for yourself, please reach out to someone you trust or a grief-informed professional. If you are having thoughts of ending your life, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
